Description: Capabilities are realized by Core Services (NAF NCV-2: Capability-to-Service mapping)
Capabilities are realized by COI Services (NAF NCV-2: domain-specific realization)
Capabilities are realized by Communications Services (NAF NCV-2: communication capabilities)
Core Services support Business Processes (TOGAF Business Architecture: service enablement)
COI Services support Business Processes (domain-specific process support)
Communications Services support Business Processes (communication infrastructure for processes)
Communications Services support Core Services (network/transport layer for services)
Business Processes produce Information Products (TOGAF Data Architecture: process output)
Business Processes consume Information Products (TOGAF Data Architecture: process input)
User Applications use Core Services (NAF NSV-1: application-service binding)
User Applications use COI Services (end-user access to COI services)
User Applications use Communications Services (end-user communication binding)
COI Services fulfill Capabilities (NAF NCV-5: service-to-capability assignment)
Core Services fulfill Capabilities (NAF NCV-5: generic service fulfillment)
Business Roles are assigned to Business Processes (TOGAF Org Mapping: role responsibility)
Core Services depend on COI Services (technical dependency: base services to domain)
COI Services depend on Core Services (technical dependency: domain to base services)
Communications Services depend on Core Services (network requires platform services)
User Applications consume Information Products (end-user data visualization)
Communications Services communicate with COI Services (NAF NSOV: communication compound)
Communications Services communicate with User Applications (network binding of applications)
Business Processes are mapped to Capabilities (business-capability alignment)
Business Roles relate to Capabilities (role competencies to capabilities)
Information Products relate to Capabilities (information support of capabilities)
